senses.gloss: Hud, a prophet of Islam, sometimes identified with Eber in the Bible
Hud, the eleventh sura of the Qur'an

examples.text: وَقَالُوا كُونُوا هُودًا أَوْ نَصَارَىٰ تَهْتَدُوا
examples.english: They say, "Be Jews or Christians [so] you will be guided."

examples.text: كُلُّ مَوْلُودٍ يُولَدُ عَلَى الْفِطْرَةِ، فَأَبَوَاهُ يُهَوِّدَانِهِ أَوْ يُنَصِّرَانِهِ أَوْ يُمَجِّسَانِهِ
examples.english: Every newborn is born upon the fitra; its parents then Judaize or Christianize or Zoroastrianize it.

senses.gloss: to make someone drunk, to intoxicate someone, to inebriate someone, to souse someone

etymology.text:
Uncertain. Probably from the root ه و د (h w d), related to returning. See هاد (hād). Otherwise, it might be related to ه د ي (h d y), related to guidance. See هادٍ (hādin).

Borrowed from a different Aramaic dialect than from which يَهُودِيّ (yahūdiyy) was borrowed, where the anlauting yə part was weakened to i or disappeared. No singulative is attested and the form was obsolete already at the time of the Qurʾān, only used formulaically.

Denominal verb of هُود (hūd), with the second triliteral conjugation implying the bestowing of a particular quality.
